items.title: Can NATO survive Trump's changes of heart?
items.description: These days, every official visit, every summit that involves the President of the United States seems like a mad scramble to keep the leader of the Atlantic Alliance on board. But what if it’s just too late? Last month, leaders left France thanking their lucky stars that Donald Trump hadn’t taken a big bazooka to the G7 Summit.In Ankara, a darker Donald threatening to pull all US troops from Europe, cut trade with Spain and blasting Nato for its unwillingness to join his war against Iran. He claimed he wouldn’t have even shown up but for his friend Recep Tayep Erdogan hosting. Putting put-downs and popularity contests aside, real lives are at stake. How do allies go faster in providing defence systems against Russia's cruise missiles lobbed on Ukraine? What if Vladimir Putin, whose citizens are feeling the pinch from Ukrainian drones targeting power and fuel supplies, decides to go for broke and test the resolve of frontline European states?

items.title: Former rebel turned government ally proposes a path to peace for Sudan
items.description: Malik Agar Ayyir has proposed a national dialogue to end the civil war and steer Sudan towards democratic civilian rule.

items.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time

items.title: Why Russia is trying to jam Starlink and what it means for Ukraine | Explained
items.description: Starlink has become a crucial tool for Ukraine’s military, enabling battlefield communications and long-range drone operations. Now, Russia is deploying advanced electronic warfare systems to jam Starlink signals and disrupt Ukrainian attacks.

items.title: Trump to ask US Supreme Court for new hearing on birthright citizenship
items.description: In June, the Supreme Court ruled against Trump’s order to end birthright citizenship for individuals born in the US.

items.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
items.description: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

items.description: Iranian state media has released what it calls the first footage showing damage to the late Khamenei’s compound.

items.title: The state of the NATO alliance amid Trump's second term
items.description: Carl Bildt, co-chair of the European Council on Foreign Relations, talks about the ongoing NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, and the state of the alliance during President Trump's second term.

items.title: World Cup 2026: France-Morocco refereeing already sparks debate as Deschamps responds
items.description: The referee for France's World Cup quarter-final against Morocco is already drawing attention. FIFA has appointed Argentine official Facundo Tello to oversee the match, a decision that has prompted widespread discussion. Asked about the appointment, France coach Didier Deschamps refused to fuel the debate, saying he trusts the refereeing team.

items.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
items.description: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

items.title: Moroccan police arrest 10 people over thwarted Islamic State-linked attack plots
items.description: Morocco's counterterrorism agency on Monday said it had foiled several attack plots by a cell linked to the Islamic State affiliate in the Sahel.

items.title: Can Fremantle keep rolling to the AFL minor premiership?
items.description: Fremantle has lost its 14-game winning streak with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

items.title: Iran war: Trump says ceasefire 'over' after fresh strikes
items.description: The US president said it's a "waste of time" dealing with Tehran after the US struck dozens of Iranian targets overnight and again later on Wednesday.

items.title: 'The water just came so fast': Typhoon triggers floods and rare tornadoes in China
items.description: Villagers whose families have been stranded told the BBC they are struggling to get food and help.

items.title: In Bordeaux's brand new eco-district of Brazza, homes turn into ovens during the heatwave
items.description: France is experiencing its third heatwave in two months. In Bordeaux the thermometre hit 40 degrees Celsius this week, including in its new eco-neighbourhood. The apartment blocs are new, built to the latest standards - but inside it’s like an oven, and residents have had enough. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Iran war: Trump calls second set of US strikes 'retribution'
items.description: The United States launched fresh strikes on Iran on Wednesday night, the second wave of strikes in 24 hours after President Trump declared a ceasefire to be over. DW has more.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
items.description: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

items.title: Platner's disastrous candidacy exposes rifts that could dampen Democrats' Senate hopes
items.description: Along with creating tumult in a must-win race for Democrats, Graham Platner's exit is laying bare division between the party's left wing and moderates.

items.description: The strikes came hours after U.S. President Donald Trump said recent Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz signalled the end of the fragile ceasefire.

items.description: The wreckage was recovered from 53 NM South of ORMARA, it said, adding that efforts are under way to find the five missing crew members

items.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov began his African tour with a visit to Ethiopia on Tuesday, where he met with Mahamoud Ali Youssouf, chairperson of the African Union Commission.

items.title: Ebola treatment facilities in DRC race to adapt to growing patient needs
items.description: In response to the spread of the Ebola epidemic caused by the Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, patient care capacity has been significantly strengthened in Ituri province.

items.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
items.description: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

items.title: Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
items.description: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

items.title: Is the U.S.-Iran agreement falling apart?
items.description: There were two major hurdles in the implementation of the MoU signed on June 17. One was the war in Lebanon and the other was the status of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Kenya 'Birdman' is internet hit but worries health officials
items.description: A formerly homeless man in Nairobi has become a viral sensation after years of rescuing and living with injured birds, but authorities are worried about disease risks.

items.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000

items.title: East German doping victims still lack adequate support
items.description: Thousands of athletes in former East Germany were subjected to state-sponsored doping — and many still suffer lasting health effects. But support for victims is not enough, German commissioner says.

items.title: Trump's support for Venezuela opposition head Machado wanes
items.description: Venezuelan opposition leader and Nobel Peace laureate María Corina Machado says she wants to return to her earthquake-ravaged homeland. Yet neither Venezuela's government nor the United States thinks that's a good idea.

items.title: At least five children die in monsoon landslide at a Rohingya camp in Bangladesh
items.description: More than 1 million refugees live in the camp; dollar Tripura, the local chief of the Fire Service and Civil Defence, said the landslide hit as children were attending classes

items.title: Germany: Record debt and a seismic policy shift
items.description: The German government's draft budget for 2027 includes more money for the military and less for everything else, while debt continues to grow rapidly.

items.description: Trump departed the NATO summit on an older Air Force One after arriving aboard the new Qatar-gifted aircraft.

items.title: Refugee teacher fights to keep education alive for children displaced by Sudan's war
items.description: Sudanese families who fled from the war, are living in poor conditions in the town of Tajoura in northwestern Libya are trying to make sure the children in the community don't miss out on their education.

items.title: Argentina seek to shake off 'Messi-dependence' against Mo Salah's Egypt
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side escaped a major scare against Cape Verde, but their dependence on Lionel Messi remains a growing concern ahead of a last-16 meeting with Mohamed Salah's Egypt. Leer más

items.title: DR Congo: Tensions mount over planned constitutional referendum
items.description: Tensions are rising in DR Congo over plans to introduce a new constitution. The opposition accuses President Tshisekedi of using it to seek a third term but has postponed planned protests following an AU mediation offer.

items.title: Major shipping carriers resume some sailings through Suez Canal
items.description: Danish shipping giant A.P. Moller Maersk said on Monday it will resume some sailings through the Suez Canal under the joint network with its German peer Hapag-Lloyd, instead of sending vessels around Africa's Cape of Good Hope.

items.title: Jake answers the call and saddles up for ride of a lifetime
items.description: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.

items.title: Renewed US-Iran war would hit Gulf countries hard
items.description: After US strikes on Iran, Iran targeted US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. Even if Iran isn't targeting the governments or populations of its neighbors, Gulf countries have much to lose as fighting escalates.
